---
title: "engram"
subtitle: "NeuroAI → Brain–Computer Interfaces 🧠"
title-block-banner: true
page-layout: full
listing:
  - id: featured
    contents:
      - "braindecode/shallow-convnet.ipynb"
    type: grid
    image-placeholder: assets/engram-card.svg
    fields: [image, title, description, date, categories, reading-time]
  - id: notebooks
    contents:
      - "**/*.ipynb"
      - "!cookbook.ipynb"
      - "!braindecode/shallow-convnet.ipynb"
    type: grid
    sort: "date desc"
    categories: cloud
    feed: true
    image-placeholder: assets/engram-card.svg
    fields: [image, title, description, date, categories, reading-time]
---

An **engram** is the physical trace a memory leaves in the brain 🧠 — so this is
where I store what I learn. I work in NeuroAI: I take raw neural signal and build
the models that turn it into insight 💡. Each entry here is a runnable notebook —
a paper reimplemented, a model dissected, an idea I was curious enough to chase
down. I'm building toward brain–computer interfaces 🤖, and this is the
groundwork, done in the open. 🚀

## ⭐ Featured

::: {#featured}
:::

## 📓 Notebooks

::: {#notebooks}
:::
